title: P2: GetStatus action times out — adapter not responding to status requests
description:
## Evidence
Agent log 2026-06-29 22:24:24: `GetStatus TIMED OUT after 10.4s — no result event received`. Multiple GetStatus actions are dispatched but never complete.

The adapter's `status` action case calls `sendBotStatus()` which should send a `status` event back to C#. If `sendBotStatus` throws or the event isn't sent, the C# correlation tracker never sees a resolution.

## Impact
- Inventory never syncs with the actual Minecraft inventory
- `IsComplete` checks on goals are deferred indefinitely ("inventory stale")
- Build goals can never complete because inventory is always stale

## Files
- `MineflayerAdapter/index.js` — case 'status' in dispatch switch
- `MineflayerAdapter/index.js` — sendBotStatus function

comments.author: anonymous
comments.body:
**Analysis (Sprint 56 Wave A):**

`GetStatusTool` sends action `ActionProtocol.Status` which is `"status"`. The adapter's dispatch switch has `case 'status':` which calls `sendBotStatus()` → sends event `"status"` → C# parses as `StatusEvent` → handler calls `CompleteCorrelatedActionByTool("GetStatus")` AND `CompleteCorrelatedActionByTool("Status")`.

The scope bug (`botPos`/`sendBotStatus` inside `registerBotEventHandlers()`) was already fixed in commit `aa4af10`.

TSK-0270's `ActionCompletedEvent` fix also helps here — the `actionCompleted` event (sent after all dispatch cases via the finally-adjacent try block) now acts as a safety net. If the `status` domain event is somehow dropped, `actionCompleted` will complete the correlation.

This issue likely shares root cause with TSK-0270 (stale `_stopRequested` flag, or actionCompleted not completing correlations). Awaiting live validation after TSK-0270 fix.
